findings.title: timingSafeEqual DoS — buffer length mismatch crashes server
findings.description: crypto.timingSafeEqual throws RangeError if buffers have different lengths. An attacker can send a 1-byte CSRF header to crash the server (unhandled exception = process exit).
findings.impact: Server crash (DoS). Every authenticated endpoint is affected.

findings.title: Refresh token stored and queried in plain text
findings.description: Session model stores refreshToken as plain text (@unique) AND refreshTokenHash. Token lookup uses the plain text column. If database is compromised, all active sessions can be hijacked.
findings.impact: Full session takeover on DB breach. Violates OWASP token storage best practices.

steps: 3. In refreshTokens(): hash incoming token, query by refreshTokenHash
5. Invalidate all existing sessions (delete all from sessions table)

findings.title: Regex-based XSS sanitizer bypassable
findings.description: The sanitizeValue() regex strips
findings.impact: Stored XSS via admin config — affects ALL public pages that render config values.

findings.title: Same bypassable regex XSS sanitizer (duplicate)
findings.description: Identical sanitizeValue() function duplicated from config.ts. Same bypass vectors apply to about features and team member names/roles.
findings.patch: Same fix as SEC-003. Extract sanitizeValue to shared utility.

findings.title: google_maps_embed key allows arbitrary HTML storage
findings.description: The 'google_maps_embed' config key passes through regex sanitizer which strips <iframe> — meaning legitimate embeds are stripped. But other HTML (SVG, img with handlers) can pass through.
findings.impact: Stored XSS on contact page if this value is injected via innerHTML.
findings.patch: Validate as URL only (must match https://www.google.com/maps/embed*). Have frontend build the <iframe> tag from the validated URL.

findings.title: Login accepts 6-char passwords, change-password requires 8+
findings.description: Inconsistent password requirements. Accounts created with weak 6-char passwords remain valid forever since login schema doesn't enforce the stricter policy.
findings.impact: Brute force easier on legacy accounts.

findings.title: JWT fallback secret 'dev-secret-replace-me' usable in staging
findings.description: Production guard exits process if NODE_ENV=production and no JWT_SECRET. But staging/test environments sharing production data could use the weak default.
findings.impact: Token forgery in non-production environments with real data.
findings.patch: Require JWT_SECRET in all environments except NODE_ENV=development.

findings.title: Logout endpoint unauthenticated
findings.description: POST /auth/logout accepts any refresh token without verifying the requester's identity. Combined with SEC-002, allows mass session invalidation.
findings.impact: DoS via mass logout if tokens are exposed.
findings.patch: Add requireAuth middleware to logout route.

findings.title: In-memory rate limiting and brute-force protection
findings.description: Both rate limiter (express-rate-limit) and brute-force tracker (Map) use in-memory storage. Resets on server restart. Doesn't work across multiple instances.
findings.impact: Brute force protection ineffective in multi-instance or after restarts.

findings.title: Temp files not cleaned up on Cloudinary failure or success
findings.description: When Cloudinary upload returns null (failure), the temp file in /assets/uploads/ is not deleted. Also, successful uploads leave the temp file on disk permanently.
findings.impact: Disk space exhaustion over time (slow DoS).
findings.patch: Add fs.unlinkSync(filePath) after Cloudinary upload (both success and failure paths).

findings.title: PostgreSQL and Redis ports exposed to all interfaces (0.0.0.0)
findings.description: Both 5432 and 6379 are mapped to 0.0.0.0, accessible from any network. Redis has no authentication.
findings.impact: Unauthorized database/cache access from any machine on the network.
findings.patch: Bind to localhost: '127.0.0.1:5432:5432' or remove ports entirely (server connects via Docker network).

findings.title: Redis running without authentication (requirepass)
findings.description: Redis is accessible without any password. Any process on the host can connect and execute commands.
findings.impact: Cache poisoning, data exfiltration, RCE via Redis EVAL.
findings.patch:
Add: command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD:-redispass}
And add REDIS_URL with password to server environment.

findings.title: CSP allows unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval
findings.description: Content Security Policy includes 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' in script-src. This weakens XSS protection significantly.
findings.impact: Inline scripts and eval() allowed — reduces XSS mitigation from CSP.
findings.patch: For production: use nonce-based CSP. For MVP: document the limitation.

findings.title: .dockerignore in wrong directory — all files sent to Docker daemon
findings.description: Build context is project root (.) but .dockerignore is in server/. Docker only reads .dockerignore from build context root. Result: node_modules, .env, backups, FluxPay files all sent to daemon.
findings.impact: Bloated Docker image, secrets potentially in build cache.

findings.title: Default admin credentials exposed in public HTML
findings.description: The post-purchase page shows admin@restaurante.com and password admin123 in plain HTML visible to anyone.
findings.impact: Anyone who guesses the URL can see default credentials.
findings.patch: Remove credentials from HTML. Send via email after purchase, or show only to authenticated buyer.

findings.title: Database credentials hardcoded in compose file
findings.description: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres in plain text. Users who don't change this run with default credentials in production.
findings.patch: Use env_file or env vars: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-postgres}

findings.title: CSRF cookie has httpOnly: false
findings.description: The CSRF cookie is intentionally accessible to JavaScript (double-submit pattern requires it). This is by design but worth documenting.
findings.impact: None — this is the correct behavior for double-submit CSRF.
findings.patch: No fix needed. Document the design decision.

findings.title: MIME type check relies on client-sent Content-Type header
findings.description: The initial MIME filter can be bypassed by spoofing Content-Type. However, magic byte validation in the route handler catches this.
findings.impact: None — defense in depth is present. Magic bytes are the real guard.
findings.patch: No fix needed. The triple-check (MIME + extension + magic bytes) is solid.

findings.title: Missing @@index([userId]) on Session model
findings.description: Session queries by userId (findMany, deleteMany) are full table scans without an index.
findings.impact: Performance degradation with many sessions. Not a security risk directly.

blockers_for_production: SEC-001: CSRF DoS — server crashes on malformed token
SEC-002: Plain text refresh tokens in database
must_fix_before_clients: SEC-003/SEC-004: Replace regex XSS sanitizer with sanitize-html
SEC-011: Bind DB/Redis ports to localhost
